name: Ceridwen Extended
description: Ceridwen, a powerful and enigmatic figure in Welsh mythology, is primarily known as a sorceress and goddess associated with wisdom, magic, transformation, fertility, and the Underworld. She is the mother of Morfran (Afagddu) and Creirwy, and her most famous tale revolves around her attempts to grant her son wisdom through a magical potion. Ceridwen possessed a cauldron, called Amen, in which she brewed this potion for a year and a day. Morda, a blind man, stoked the fire beneath the cauldron, and Gwion Bach, a young boy, stirred the mixture. Three drops of the potion granted immense wisdom, but the remaining contents were poisonous. Gwion accidentally tasted the three drops and gained the wisdom Ceridwen intended for her son. Enraged, Ceridwen pursued Gwion, who transformed into various animals in an attempt to escape. Ceridwen, in turn, transformed into corresponding animals, ultimately catching him when he became a grain of wheat and she a hen. She swallowed him, becoming pregnant. When she gave birth, she could not bring herself to kill him, so she placed him in a leather bag and cast him into the sea. He was later rescued and grew up to become the great bard Taliesin. Ceridwen represents the transformative power of knowledge, the cycles of life and death, and the potent forces of nature. She is often depicted as a formidable figure, embodying both maternal care and fierce determination. Her cauldron symbolizes transformation, abundance, and the mysteries of the Otherworld. She is also associated with prophecy and inspiration. Her story serves as a reminder that even unintended consequences can lead to extraordinary outcomes and that wisdom can be found in unexpected places.
subtitle: Goddess of Magic, Transformation, and Wisdom

keyMyths.title: The Brewing of the Cauldron
keyMyths.description: Ceridwen brews a potion of wisdom for her son, but Gwion accidentally tastes it and gains the knowledge instead. This sets off a chase where both transform into different animals.

keyMyths.title: The Chase and Rebirth of Taliesin
keyMyths.description: Ceridwen chases Gwion, both transforming into various animals. She eventually swallows him as a grain of wheat, becoming pregnant and giving birth to Taliesin.

keyMyths.title: Mother of Morfran and Creirwy
keyMyths.description: Ceridwen is the mother of the beautiful Creirwy and the unsightly Morfran (Afagddu). This parentage highlights her complex nature and motivations.

sources.author: Lady Charlotte Guest
